# Blindspot Pass

The named, conditional opening move that surfaces suspected blind spots — `[UU]` entries — before grilling starts. Grilling can only clear questions someone already knows to ask; the blindspot pass is the reconnaissance step for what nobody in the session knows to ask yet.

## Trigger

Shaping in territory where the shaper lacks fluency: a new domain, an unfamiliar subsystem, a first collaboration with this codebase or team. Recognize the signal in yourself, not just the user — if you're guessing at conventions rather than recalling them, the territory is unfamiliar.

## Offered, Never Imposed

Ask the user whether to run it; skip when they're the domain expert. Do not auto-run it based on a heuristic guess at unfamiliarity ("this seems complex, so run it") — that just hides the same judgment call inside the skill instead of surfacing it to the person who can actually answer "am I the expert here?"

## Prompt Shape

Ask, against the specific territory named by the Change:

> What would I not know to ask here — codebase history, domain conventions, prior art, potholes?

Disclose the shaper's own starting point alongside the question — what they already know, so the answer targets the actual gap instead of restating the obvious. A blindspot pass run without disclosing context produces generic caution, not a targeted finding.

## Deliverable

Fog entries with quadrant tags. Nothing else — never conclusions, never scope decisions. The pass's job is to name what's unknown, not to decide what to do about it; the human adjudicates every entry it returns.

A `[UU]` that gets named through this pass becomes:

- a `[KU]` (state it precisely enough to grill) or
- a `[UK]` (you'd recognize the right answer on sight — build a reaction artifact)

and re-routes through the quadrant table in the main skill body.

## Stopping

The pass ends when the shaper (or the user) can name the territory's remaining risks as entries, not vague unease. A pass that keeps surfacing "something might be wrong here" without a nameable entry has run past its useful length — stop and proceed with what's been named.
